title: Deja vu coming back from Eugene through Springfield
html:
TL;DR

  • Sept 23, 2019 micro-note: Yahir gets a déjà vu hit while driving back from Eugene through Springfield, triggered by opening a Snapchat photo (image attachment preserved in raw, not surfaced).
  • Single-image apple note logging an altered perception trigger — mid-route between two Oregon cities, mid-Snap-open — with no further commentary.
  • This is the second-known entry where Yahir treats a déjà vu event as worth timestamping (compare with later 2021–2022 dream-log entries).
  • Geographic context: Yahir is a Grants Pass high-schooler making the I-5 corridor trip — Eugene/Springfield is the midway-north waypoint.
  • "Snapchat photo" implies the trigger was a piece of fleeting social-media content reactivating a prior memory — proto-version of the trigger spotting habit.

Key fact

  • Note title is the entire content. The body is a single image-attachment placeholder (preserved in immutable raw).

Why it matters in the graph

The note is short but it's the earliest dated déjà vu log in the vault. Yahir is already in 2019 doing the thing the later codifies — capturing a perception-glitch the moment it happens, on the road, mid-action. The triggering medium (Snapchat) is itself temporally compressed (24-hour disappearing photos), which makes the déjà vu hit doubly recursive: a memory-flash triggered by a memory-medium.

The route — Grants Pass → Eugene → Springfield — is the physical geography of his pre-OSU teen mobility radius along Oregon's I-5 corridor.

Wikilinks
